text: Object Oriented Programming (OOP) Dr. Abdurrahman A. Nasr Al-Azhar University Lecture 2 JDK and Data types Java Development Kit (JDK) • java: Serves as a Java interpreter used to run Java applications by reading and interpreting bytecode files • javac: Serves as a Java compiler used to translate Java source code files. • javadoc: Creates HTML documentation for Java source code files • javap: Serves as a Java disassemble used to convert bytecode files into a Java program description • jdb: Serves as a Java debugger used to find errors in Java programs • jar: Serves as archive used to package related class libraries into a single executable JAR file. = Java Virtual Machine (JVM)

Java Tokens Smallest individual element in java Keywords Identifiers Literals Constants Separators Operators l Java Tokens: Keywords Not to be used as identifiers List of reserved words for java compiler Java Tokens: Identifiers Symbolic name that refers to name of variables, methods, classes, packages, interfaces Consist of alphabets, numbers, underscore(_), and the dollar sign $ Can’t start with number Case sensitive, so move and Move are 2 different identifiers Camel case

" Java Tokens: Literals Value assigned to a variable Can be changed depending on program requirement Based on the datatype, the literals can be ° Integer Literals • Floating Point Literals • Boolean Literals 24 124.543 true |l v ] "hello” // an int literal // a double literal // boolean literal // a char literal // a string literal • Character Literals Insert a tab in the text at this point. W \ / Operators Boolean logical » These operators operate on boolean operands only ° Logical AND {&) o Logical OR ([} 0 Logical XOR (%) Bitwise 0 0 0 0 0 0 Logical Unary NOT (1) Short circuit AND (&&) Short circuit OR (][ AND assignment (&=) OR assignment ([=) XOR assignment (=) » These operators perform on the individual bits of their operands ° AND (&) L- - - OR () XOR () Right Shift (>>) Left Shift(<<) 0 Equal to (==) © Not Equal to {1=) Increment and Decrement = Unary operators. There are two kinds, prefix and postfix. In prefix method, the value of the operand is incremented or decremented before assigning the postfix method the value of the operand is incremented or decremented after assigning it to a variable. in o Increment(++x, x++) o Decrement(-x, x—) Conditional « |tis aternary

L= default @ //Optional Statements WHILE When executing, if the boolean_expression result is true then the actions inside the loop will be executed. This will DO-WHILE The Boolean expression appears at the end of the loop, so the statements in the loop execute once before the Boolean do, and the statements in the loop execute again. This process repeats until the Boolean expression is false. oot contin FOR FOR EACH Stack and Heap ’ Stack and Heap
